calendar.matters:
SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-08-2021 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Short Cause Trial/Prove-Up Hearing as to cross-defendant CER Mechanical Corp., a California corporation.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Daniel Rappaport, Esq. appeared for cross-complainant Eric F. Anderson, Inc.
Kristin Anderson, President and CEO of Eric F. Anderson, Inc., a California corporation, was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by cross-defendant CER Mechanical Corp., a California corporation.

Cross-complainant's complaint for Breach of Contract was filed on July 25, 2019.
Cross-defendant Eric F. Anderson's answer to the cross-complaint was filed on August 26, 2019.
Notice of Time & Place of Trial of the complaint and cross-complaint was served on November 6, 2019, setting trial for June 1, 2021.
On June 1, 2020, Department 206 continued the trial to March 1, 2021. On that date, Department 206 assigned the matter to Department 514 for Short Cause Trial/Prove-Up hearing on the cross-complaint, and DISMISSED the underlying complaint with prejudice, for CER Mechanical's failure to appear at trial call.
ROE cross-defendants were dismissed on March 5, 2021.

Cross-complainant Eric F. Anderson, Inc. submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit A: Cross-Complaint for Breach of Contract filed 7/25/2019.....................ID/EVID
Exhibit B: Subcontract Agreement dated March 27, 2018.................................ID/EVID
Exhibit C: Diagrams depicting work completed by CER Mechanical........................ID/EVID
Exhibit D: Invoices from Delcon Heating & Air Conditioning............................ID/EVID
Exhibit E: Invoices from Roundtree Heating & Plumbing.................................ID/EVID
Exhibit F: Subcontract Change Order dated January 7, 2019.............................ID/EVID
Exhibit G: Change Order Request dated February 19, 2019...............................ID/EVID
Exhibit H: Invoice from Sontoya Corporation dated October 4, 2018.....................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of cross-complainant Eric F. Anderson, Inc., a California corporation, and against cross-defendant CER Mechanical Corp., a California corporation, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $272,253.53.
Prejudgment Interest - $52,063.83.
Costs - $570.00.

Total - $324,887.36.

Counsel for cross-complainant shall prepared the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Cross-complainant STIPULATES to the return of the exhibits.

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-18-2021 Continued To Apr-08-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-08-2021 Continued To Mar-22-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 514

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2021 Assigned To Trial Court

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2021 Off Calendar

Eric F. Anderson, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order That Matters In Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. PATRICK J. WHITEHORN, Esq. of Last & Faoro (Tel: 650-696-8350) appearing for Plaintiff CER Mechanical Corp.;
Atty. MIGUEL SALDANA, Esq. of Wendel Rosen LLP (Tel: 510-834-6600) for Defendant and Cross-Complainant Eric F. Anderson, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ERIC F. ANDERSON, INC.'s Motion For An Order That Matters In Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted And For Sanctions.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications as follows: Motion granted as unopposed. Requests for Admissions, Set #1, RFA #1 - 33 are deemed admitted. Request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $3160 against plaintiff. Payment to be made to the Client Trust Fund of Wendel Rosen LLP, 1111 Broadway, 24th Floor, Oakland, CA 94607-4036.

Defendant to prepare and submit a proposed order in Word format to the Judge Pro Tem in Word format Klgallo@discoveryreferee.com.
